label: SES-068 PI-026 supersession close-out: DEC-215 supersedes DEC-206 (Option I — skip references as deposit_event_wrote_record targets — adopted at apply time after vocab.py schema-vs-spec contradiction rejected the references-as-targets POSTs); PI-046 surfaces the vocab.ENTITY_TYPES vs vocab._kinds_for_pair contradiction for the planning queue; PI-047 surfaces the ses_030 ↔ ses_036 duplicate-session artifact for formal cleanup. 1 session, 1 decision (DEC-215), 2 planning items (PI-046, PI-047), 4 references (1 supersedes, 1 decided_in, 2 is_about).

session.title: PI-026 supersession close-out — DEC-215 supersedes DEC-206 after Option I adopted at apply time; PI-046 vocab.py schema-vs-spec contradiction and PI-047 ses_030 / ses_036 duplicate-session artifact surfaced for the planning queue
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai ARCHITECTURE-mode follow-up to SES-066, in the same conversation. The opening turns drafted PI-026's three planning artifacts (ses_066.json, the apply prompt, and the backfill prompt) and applied them via the orchestrator at commit fb184d5. Doug ran the orchestrator at his terminal: Step 1 (SES-066 close-out apply) landed cleanly. Step 2 (PI-026 backfill) hit a wall on the first DEP POST — the API rejected target_type="reference" with a validator error, even though the spec (close_out_payload.md, deposit_event.md) and DEC-206 both prescribed reference targets. A parallel sandbox session investigated, found the vocab.py contradiction (vocab._kinds_for_pair line 354-360 admits reference as a wrote_record target, but vocab.ENTITY_TYPES line 250-289 excludes "reference" — so the RELATIONSHIP_RULES dict comprehension at line 377-381 never generates the ("deposit_event", "reference") key, and the validator rejects the POST), and adopted Option I: skip references entirely, mirror Phase 1's pattern. The parallel session refactored the backfill script (commits 384c224 and 944495e) and the backfill landed with 96 wrote_record edges instead of 221. Doug pushed a follow-up commit 4e15c1e aligning the verify-step expected_wrote constant from the stale 221 (DEC-206-era count) to 96 so idempotent re-runs pass cleanly. Doug then reported back: both steps landed, with the Option-I deviation flagged as governance debt and two follow-up items named (vocab.py contradiction; ses_030 ↔ ses_036 duplicate). This follow-up surfaced one consequential decision in ARCHITECTURE mode — how to close the governance gap. Three options were framed (α tight follow-up close-out now; β fold into PI-023; γ defer to PI-023's run). Doug approved α with the terse "a". The close-out's authoring also hit a parallel-sandbox identifier collision: another sandbox session committed SES-067 (PI-029 slice B build planning, claiming DEC-211..214) at commit c0b898e during this work's authoring window; this close-out rebased to SES-068 / DEC-215 to avoid the collision. The fourth such collision in the recent backfill program; PI-032's Code Change Lifecycle reserve-at-apply-time identifier model is the eventual structural fix. This close-out is the deliverable: DEC-215 supersedes DEC-206 with full context capture, PI-046 and PI-047 enter the planning queue.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt (effective): "both steps now landed and pushed" followed by Doug's full results report — pre/post heads for each step, the Option-I narrative, the two open follow-ups Doug surfaced.

(1) Acknowledged Doug's results report. Step 1 (SES-066 apply) landed clean. Step 2 (PI-026 backfill) landed with the Option-I deviation: 24 COPs, 24 DEPs, 96 wrote_record edges (24 session + 63 decision + 9 planning_item), no reference targets.

(2) Identified the governance debt: the supersession itself (Option I overrode DEC-206) lives only in commit messages; the vocab.py contradiction and the ses_030/ses_036 duplicate-session artifact also lack governance homes.

(3) Verified the vocab.py contradiction directly by reading crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/vocab.py lines 211-380. Confirmed line 250-289 defines ENTITY_TYPES as a frozenset NOT including "reference". Line 354-360 has _kinds_for_pair admitting target_type="reference" for source_type="deposit_event" with kind "deposit_event_wrote_record". Line 377-381 computes RELATIONSHIP_RULES as a dict comprehension over ENTITY_TYPES × ENTITY_TYPES — so the ("deposit_event", "reference") pair is never generated; the validator's lookup against RELATIONSHIP_RULES fails.

(4) Verified relationship vocabulary supports the supersession edge. REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS includes "supersedes". _kinds_for_pair line 303-304: "if source_type == target_type: kinds.add('supersedes')". DEC-215 → DEC-206 with kind "supersedes" is valid (both "decision" type). RELATIONSHIP_RULES contains the ("decision", "decision") key.

(5) Verified database state. DEC-206 in decisions.json with status="Active". Zero existing supersedes edges for decisions (DEC-215 → DEC-206 is the first decision-supersedes-decision edge in the database). Planning items have only item_type="pending_work" in the snapshot.

(6) Surfaced the consequential decision using the eight-element template. Three options framed (α tight follow-up close-out NOW; β fold supersession into PI-023's planning; γ defer entirely to PI-023's run). Recommendation: α. Doug approved with "a".

(7) Identifier-collision rebase. During authoring, a parallel sandbox session committed c0b898e (SES-067 close-out payload + apply prompt + PI-029 slice B Claude Code prompt — PI-029 slice B build planning, claiming SES-067 / DEC-211 / DEC-212 / DEC-213 / DEC-214). This conversation's draft had used SES-067 / DEC-211; rebased to SES-068 / DEC-215. Planning items PI-046 and PI-047 were not claimed by PI-029 slice B (its payload has zero planning items); kept as PI-046 / PI-047. The supersession target DEC-206 is unaffected.

(8) Decision-and-announce items resolved inline. (a) DEC-206 stays at status="Active" rather than transitioning to a hypothetical "Superseded" status — the supersedes edge in references is the canonical signal. (b) PI-046 and PI-047 created without explicit workstream attachment, matching PI-045's pattern. (c) PI-046's resolution options enumerated but not chosen here — that's PI-046's own planning work. (d) PI-047's resolution options similarly enumerated for PI-047's planning work.

(9) Deliverables. Two artifacts: ses_068.json (this payload) and CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-068.md (the apply prompt). Both committed and pushed from the sandbox in one commit per the sandbox convention.

(10) In-flight at end. PI-023 kickoff to be authored next as a Claude.ai deliverable at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-023-workstream-state-reconciliation-utility-kickoff.md. The kickoff should explicitly call out that DEC-215 supersedes DEC-206 and that PI-023's reconciliation utility must respect supersedes edges when computing "active decisions honored by records."
session.artifacts_produced: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_068.json (this payload — bundles 1 session record SES-068, 1 decision DEC-215, 2 planning items PI-046 and PI-047, 4 references). PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-068.md (apply prompt for this payload). Both files committed and pushed from the sandbox in one commit.
session.in_flight_at_end:
PI-023 workstream-state reconciliation utility kickoff — to be authored next as a Claude.ai deliverable at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-023-workstream-state-reconciliation-utility-kickoff.md. The kickoff should explicitly call out that DEC-215 supersedes DEC-206 (this close-out's deliverable) and that PI-023's reconciliation utility must respect supersedes edges when computing "active decisions honored by records" — without that, the utility would flag the 24 PI-026 DEPs' missing reference wrote_record edges as an invariant violation against (now-superseded) DEC-206.

PI-046 (vocab.py schema-vs-spec contradiction) and PI-047 (ses_030 ↔ ses_036 duplicate-session) enter the planning queue at Open status without workstream attachment. Both are eligible for inclusion in the next workstream lane that absorbs governance-data-quality cleanup work.

PI-029 slice B (parallel-sandbox SES-067, committed during this conversation's authoring window at c0b898e) has its own apply prompt and should be applied independently per its own planning conversation's deliverable. Apply order between SES-067 and SES-068 is not constrained — the COP identifiers derive from filename (ses_067.json → COP-067; ses_068.json → COP-068) and don't collide; DEP identifiers are assigned in apply order from the next-identifier helper.

The parallel-sandbox identifier-collision pattern continues to accumulate (this is the fourth occurrence in the recent backfill program). The eventual structural fix is PI-032's Code Change Lifecycle reserve-at-apply-time identifier model. Until then, every Claude.ai close-out conversation should expect to rebase against late-landing parallel-sandbox commits.

decisions.title: Option I (skip references as deposit_event_wrote_record targets, mirror Phase 1's pattern) supersedes DEC-206 after apply-time discovery of a vocab.py schema-vs-spec contradiction
decisions.context:
DEC-206 prescribed that PI-026 author deposit_event_wrote_record edges with target_type="reference" for the 125 resolvable references across the 24 historical close-outs. The decision's rationale rested on a spec read — close_out_payload.md and deposit_event.md both describe wrote_record as covering "sessions, decisions, planning items, and references." vocab.py's _kinds_for_pair at lines 354-360 admits reference as a valid target_type for source_type="deposit_event" with kind "deposit_event_wrote_record". The decision was authored and applied (in SES-066) without anyone running the backfill script against the API as a smoke test.

At apply time (Step 2 of the orchestrator at commit fb184d5), the first DEP POST that tried to attach a wrote_record edge to a reference target was rejected by the API's validator with a 422 error citing an invalid target_type. The vocab.py contradiction surfaced: ENTITY_TYPES (lines 250-289) does NOT include "reference"; the RELATIONSHIP_RULES dict comprehension at line 379 iterates over ENTITY_TYPES × ENTITY_TYPES, so the ("deposit_event", "reference") key is never generated; the validator's lookup against RELATIONSHIP_RULES fails. The _kinds_for_pair admission is speculative — it admits the kind for the future case where "reference" enters ENTITY_TYPES, but the validator's dict lookup is the binding gate.

A parallel sandbox session investigated, refactored the backfill script to Option I (skip references entirely, mirror Phase 1's pattern, leave the schema-vs-spec contradiction as future work), and ran the backfill (commits 384c224 and 944495e). All 24 PI-026 COPs and 24 DEPs landed; the DEPs carry records_summary.references=0 and zero reference-target wrote_record edges. Total wrote_record edges authored: 96 (24 session + 63 decision + 9 planning_item), not the 221 that DEC-206 prescribed.
decisions.decision: Option I is the binding implementation: PI-026's 24 backfilled DEPs do not carry deposit_event_wrote_record edges with target_type="reference". records_summary.references on each PI-026 DEP is 0. The pattern symmetrically mirrors Phase 1's 8 reference-orphan DEPs (DEP-001..008). DEC-215 supersedes DEC-206 via a supersedes edge in references (DEC-215 → DEC-206, kind="supersedes"). DEC-206 itself stays at status="Active" in the decisions table — the supersedes edge is the canonical override signal, not a status field transition. Downstream consumers (audit queries, PI-023's reconciliation utility, future readers) must respect supersedes edges when computing "active decisions honored by records."
decisions.rationale: Implementation reality trumps spec aspiration when the schema doesn't admit the spec's prescribed behavior. The cheap path forward — fix the schema-vs-spec contradiction in vocab.py and retry — was rejected at apply time because (a) the fix requires a schema migration plus downstream cascades (CHECK constraint extension, dialog rule updates, possibly status enum additions), all well beyond PI-026's scope; and (b) Phase 1's identical references-orphan pattern is permanent under deposit_event's born-terminal append-only lifecycle anyway, so fixing the schema doesn't unblock retroactive amendment of Phase 1's records. Option I keeps PI-026 in scope, lands its 24 DEPs cleanly, and preserves the Phase 1 reference-orphan pattern as a consistent historical-backfill convention. The schema-vs-spec contradiction is a real bug that deserves a planning item (PI-046, surfaced in this same close-out) but not an in-line fix during PI-026's apply window.
decisions.alternatives_considered:
(1) Schema migration in-line — add "reference" to ENTITY_TYPES, extend the CHECK constraints ck_ref_source_type and ck_ref_target_type via Alembic migration, regenerate RELATIONSHIP_RULES at module load, retry the PI-026 backfill. Rejected at apply time on scope grounds: schema migrations are not part of PI-026's deliverable; the downstream cascade is non-trivial; and Phase 1's reference-orphan pattern is permanent regardless so the asymmetry described in DEC-210's consequences is unavoidable for the historical graph.

(2) Leave DEC-206 active and let PI-023's reconciliation utility surface the contradiction as a finding — equivalent to option γ in the consequential-decision framing of this follow-up close-out. Rejected per Doug's α approval: DEC-206 sitting Active without a supersession edge means audit queries between now and PI-023's run see a fake invariant violation rather than a known-historical-override.

(3) Transition DEC-206 to a hypothetical "Superseded" status. Rejected as a decide-and-announce: the decisions schema does not define such a status; the supersedes edge in references is the canonical signal; introducing a status enum value is scope creep.
decisions.consequences:
PI-026's 24 backfilled DEPs carry records_summary.references=0 and zero wrote_record edges for reference targets. Mirrors Phase 1's DEP-001..008 reference-orphan pattern symmetrically — the asymmetry described in DEC-210's consequences (Phase 1 orphan, PI-026 full coverage) no longer applies; both phases skip references uniformly.

PI-023's workstream-state reconciliation utility (to be designed next) must respect supersedes edges when computing "active decisions honored by records." The naive implementation (DEC-X is Active AND records contradict DEC-X → invariant violation) would flag the 24 PI-026 DEPs against DEC-206; the correct implementation traverses supersedes edges first and reports against the latest-in-chain decision (DEC-215 in this case, which the records honor).

PI-046 (the schema-vs-spec contradiction) enters the planning queue. Whichever option PI-046 chooses (add "reference" to ENTITY_TYPES vs remove the speculative admission from _kinds_for_pair) determines whether wrote_record-to-reference edges become POST-able in the future. If PI-046 chooses to admit them, future close-out applies from the real-time path will carry reference-target wrote_record edges; PI-026's historical records remain orphan (immutable). If PI-046 chooses to remove the admission, the spec's wrote_record description in close_out_payload.md and deposit_event.md needs amendment to drop "references" from the target-type list — that's PI-046's call.

planning_items.title: Resolve vocab.py schema-vs-spec contradiction for reference targets in deposit_event_wrote_record edges
planning_items.description:
vocab._kinds_for_pair at lines 354-360 admits target_type="reference" as a valid target for source_type="deposit_event" with kind="deposit_event_wrote_record". vocab.ENTITY_TYPES at lines 250-289 does NOT include "reference" — so RELATIONSHIP_RULES (the dict computed at line 377-381 as the cartesian product of ENTITY_TYPES × ENTITY_TYPES with valid kinds) never generates the ("deposit_event", "reference") key. The API's POST validator reads RELATIONSHIP_RULES; POSTs with target_type="reference" fail the dict lookup and return HTTP 422. The two specs (close_out_payload.md and deposit_event.md) describe wrote_record as covering "sessions, decisions, planning items, and references" — the spec aligns with _kinds_for_pair's admission but is contradicted by ENTITY_TYPES.

Discovered at PI-026's apply time (per DEC-215's context). Resolution options: (A) Add "reference" to vocab.ENTITY_TYPES. Cascades: extend the CHECK constraints ck_ref_source_type and ck_ref_target_type in an Alembic migration (admit "reference" as a valid type); the v0.3 cascading ReferenceCreateDialog inherits the change automatically via RELATIONSHIP_RULES regeneration. Consequence: future real-time close-out applies will carry wrote_record edges to reference targets; PI-026's historical DEPs remain reference-orphan (immutable per born-terminal lifecycle).
(B) Remove the "reference" admission from vocab._kinds_for_pair at lines 354-360. Update close_out_payload.md and deposit_event.md to drop "references" from the wrote_record target-type list. Consequence: the apply path symmetrically skips references for all backfilled and real-time close-outs going forward; aligns with Phase 1 and PI-026's actual pattern.
(C) Some hybrid (e.g., admit reference-target wrote_record only under specific status transitions, or carry the reference-target info in records_summary.references count without an explicit edge).

Resolution requires deciding whether reference-as-wrote_record-target adds enough audit value to justify the schema-extension cost. Phase 1 said no; PI-026's DEC-206 said yes but couldn't implement; DEC-215 says no for now. PI-023's reconciliation utility may surface additional context once it can analyze the full apply-event graph.

planning_items.title: Resolve ses_030 / ses_036 duplicate-session artifact and the 4 unresolvable references ses_030's payload claims
planning_items.description:
ses_030.json and ses_036.json on disk in PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ share the same session title ("Styling Conversation 2 — build planning: ui-PRD-v0.6..."); both reference decisions DEC-105, DEC-106, and DEC-107 in their decisions arrays. The database has both SES-030 and SES-036 as separate session records. The decided_in edges in references.json point from DEC-105/106/107 to SES-036, not SES-030. ses_030.json also claims a session-is_about-planning_item edge to PI-001 that doesn't exist in the snapshot for SES-030.

Surfaced as DEC-208's case (c) — "four payload references in ses_030.json don't resolve in either direction" — and as a future cleanup candidate in SES-066's in_flight_at_end. PI-026's backfill resolver flagged the 4 references as unresolvable (per DEC-208's forward-then-reverse-then-skip strategy) and skipped them; DEP-028 (the DEP for ses_030's apply) carries records_summary.references=0. PI-026's COP for ses_030 (COP-017) is in applied status and carries a producing_conversation edge to CONV-027 — so the records ARE attributed to SES-030, even though the decided_in edges in the snapshot point to SES-036.

Resolution requires reading the conversations table, the original close-out file commit timestamps, and the decision-creation timestamps to determine which session is the canonical apply. Hypotheses: (a) SES-030 was an aborted/re-tried apply attempt; SES-036 is the successful re-apply; both records should be preserved historically but one should supersede the other. (b) The two sessions are genuinely separate apply events that happened to share a title; the decided_in edges should be back-filled for SES-030 (SES-036's decided_in edges may be misattributed). (c) Some other narrative.

Resolution options once canonicality is determined: (A) Merge SES-030 into SES-036 — redirect any inbound references to SES-036 + delete SES-030 (soft-delete via the session deleted_at column). (B) Mark one as supersedes the other via a session → session supersedes edge (same-type supersession is admitted by vocab._kinds_for_pair). (C) Back-fill the 4 missing decided_in references for SES-030 if SES-030 was a real apply event with its own writes; DEP-028's records_summary.references would then need amendment — but DEP-028 is born-terminal and immutable, so this option requires accepting the records_summary undercount.
